concepts.title: Parametric equation of parabola
concepts.conceptBody:
Parametric Equation

The parametric form of the parabola ${{y}^{2}}=4ax$ is ( $\left. a{{t}^{2}},2at \right)$. i.e. $x=a{{t}^{2}},y=2at$.

You can see this by just putting the values in the equation.

Equation of the chord joining any two points on the parabola 

Let $\text{P}\left( at_{1}^{2},2a{{t}_{1}} \right),Q\left( at_{2}^{2},2a{{t}_{2}} \right)$ be any two points on the parabola ${{y}^{2}}=4ax$. Then, the

equation of the chord joining these points is

$\text{y}-2\text{at}{{\text{t}}_{1}}=\frac{2\text{at}{{\text{t}}_{2}}-2\text{at}}{\text{at}t_{2}^{2}-at_{1}^{2}}\left( \text{x}-\text{att}_{1}^{2} \right)$

or $\text{y}-2\text{at}{{\text{t}}_{1}}=\frac{2}{{{\text{t}}_{1}}+{{\text{t}}_{2}}}\left( \text{x}-\text{att}_{1}^{2} \right)$

or $y\left( {{t}_{1}}+{{t}_{2}} \right)=2x+2a{{t}_{1}}{{t}_{2}}$

Condition for the chord joining points having parameters ${{t}_{1}}$ AND ${{t}_{2}}$ to be a focal chord 

If the chord joining points $\left( at_{1}^{2},2a{{t}_{1}} \right)$ and $\left( at_{2}^{2},2a{{t}_{2}} \right)$ on the parabola passes

through its focus, then $\left( \text{a},0 \right)$ satisfies the equation

$\begin{align} & \,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,y({{t}_{1}}+{{t}_{2}})=2x+2a{{t}_{1}}{{t}_{2}} \\ & \Rightarrow \,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,0=\,2a+2a{{t}_{1}}{{t}_{2}} \\ & \Rightarrow \,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,{{t}_{1}}\,{{t}_{2}}=-1 \\ \end{align}$

Length of a focal chord

Let $\text{P}\left( at_{1}^{2},2a{{t}_{1}} \right)$ and $\text{Q}\left( at_{2}^{2},2a{{t}_{2}} \right)$ be the end point of a focal chord. Then, ${{t}_{1}}{{t}_{2}}=-1$.

Now

$\begin{array}{*{35}{r}} PQ & ~=\sqrt{{{\left( at_{2}^{2}-at_{1}^{2} \right)}^{2}}+{{\left( 2a{{t}_{2}}-2a{{t}_{1}} \right)}^{2}}} \\ {} & ~=a\left( {{t}_{2}}-{{t}_{1}} \right)\sqrt{{{\left( {{t}_{2}}+{{t}_{1}} \right)}^{2}}+4} \\ {} & ~=a\left( {{t}_{2}}-{{t}_{1}} \right)\sqrt{{{\left( {{t}_{2}}+{{t}_{1}} \right)}^{2}}-4{{t}_{1}}{{t}_{2}}}\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }\left[ \cdot {{t}_{1}}{{t}_{2}}=-1 \right] \\ {} & ~=a\left( {{t}_{2}}-{{t}_{1}} \right)\sqrt{{{\left( {{t}_{2}}-{{t}_{1}} \right)}^{2}}} \\ {} & ~=a{{\left( {{t}_{2}}-{{t}_{1}} \right)}^{2}} \\ \end{array}$

Thus, the length of a focal chord having parameters ${{t}_{1}}$ and ${{t}_{2}}$ for its end points is $\text{a}{{\left( {{\text{t}}_{2}}-{{\text{t}}_{1}} \right)}^{2}}$.
